name: Windows Media Creation CLI
description: Windows Media Creation CLI is a PowerShell-based tool that automates building Windows installation media on a USB drive. It supports Windows 11 (22H2-25H2) and Windows 10, with customizable architecture, language, region and edition, enabling fully automated media creation. It also supports OEM driver injection via AUTOUNATTEND or DISM, single or multi-driver packs, and can generate an installwimdrivers.csv catalog to track installed drivers.
keywords: how to create windows 11 usb with drivers
download windows media creation tool cli
install windows with oem drivers automated
create bootable usb with custom drivers
author: Niklas Rast

securityCheck.aiSummary: The codebase largely aligns with legitimate Intune/endpoint management use for offline driver provisioning (adding OEM driver packs to Windows install media). Primary security concerns are around remote code execution risk from downloaded drivers without integrity checks, and a potential undefined base URL variable that could affect URL construction. No credential or data exfiltration patterns are evident, and obfuscation is not present. Recommendations: enforce signature/checksum verification for downloaded drivers, initialize and validate all base URLs explicitly, consider signing the scripts and restricting network sources to trusted catalogs, and document the expected vendor catalogs to reduce risk of supply-chain compromise.
